calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for Janice Patricia Anacker (Petitioner).

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that venue is to be in San Francisco. The Court directed Counsel to strike the APN number and the language regarding venue from the order. The Court granted the Petition For Spousal Property, (subject to review of the revised the order). Mr. O'Sullivan will submit the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR#12891. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ashley Dunning for Matthew Anacker. Ciaran O'Sullivan for Janice Anacker (Petitioner).

The Court inquired as to where the parties wanted to go with discovery. Ms. Dunning stated that discovery should be limited for the reasons set forth on the record, that she has not seen the will contest yet, and that the matter should be set for trial in the late spring. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that the matter was just transferred from Contra Costa County on the tenth, that he just filed his will contest yesterday, and that a status date not a trial date should be set. Ms. Dunning stated that there was another proceeding relating to the trust pending in Marin, informed the Court that her client wants to be appointed special administrator, noted that this issue has been around for awhile, and that Judge Dearman previously denied their request because venue had not been determined. The Court stated that a trial date would not be set at this time and that it's focus was on the the petition for probate and the will contest. Status on discovery was set for March 18, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/Rosie Noguera.

The Parties requested a 90 day continuance. Ms. Knudsen stated that she was working on getting the Contra Costa action and the will transferred to San Francisco. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that his Client would set his petition when the matter gets transferred. The matter was continued to December 10, 2008 for status on the transfer.

Off Calendar Apr-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. Petitioner Matthew Anacker's motion for protective order re deposition subpoena to D. Kuhner is taken off calendar, in light of the stipulation filed April 21, 2008. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Letters Of Special Administration
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. GARY D. ROTHSTEIN APPEARING MATTHEW ANACKER;
ATTY. NEIL R. BARDACK APPEARING FOR JANET PATRICIA ANACKER.

MR. ROTHSTEIN IS SEEKING THE APPOINTMENT OF MATTHEW ANACKER BECAUSE THE SURVIVING WIFE OF DECEDENT FILED A PETITION OF ADMINISTRATION IN THIS COURT AND SHE WAS ALLEGING THAT DECEDENT WAS A RESIDENT OF SAN FRANCISCO AT THE TIME OF HIS DEATH. THERE WILL ALSO BE A EVIDENTIAY HEARING SCHEDULED FOR MAY 2, 2008 WITH REGARD TO THE ISSUE OF DECEDENT'S RESIDENCY. UNTIL A PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE IS APPOINTED A SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR NEEDS TO BE APPOINTED.

JANET ANACKER IS OBJECTING THE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION OF MATTHEW ANACKER QUESTIONING THE JURISDICTION OF THIS COURT. DECEDENT WAS A RESIDENT OF CONTRA COSTA AT THE TIME OF HIS DEATH. PETITIONER INTENDS TO CHALLENGE THE VALIDITY OF THAT WILL WHICH IS FILED IN CONTRA COSTA COUNTY. AFTER HEARING FROM BOTH COUNSELS, THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST OF OBJECTOR MATTHEW ANACKER TO BE APPOINTED AS SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR. THIS COURT CONTINUED THE EVIDENTIARY HEARING REGARDING VENUE IN THIS MATTER TO MAY 9, 2008 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 204. JUDGE JOHN DEARMAN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT/SAJJA RAVINANPATRICHA; REPORTER KATY BERSAMIN
